{{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. Since there are two shrines of the same name in the city, they are treated here as upper and lower sections. === Nyuu Shrine [Kami-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Kaminyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130}}Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu 378 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=two-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509}} |神事 = Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival (April 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine crest === Circular mountain and water motif. === History === According to shrine tradition, around the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, Nyuu no Mahito enshrined the Nyuu deity on Mt. Tanbono. It is said to have been moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 8). The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals === * Okonai - January 7 * Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival - April 2 * Annual festival - April 3 * Nogami Festival - August 23 === Shrine outside precincts === * Hachiman Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Sacred grove === The surrounding area is designated as the "Nyuu Shrine Sacred Grove" under Shiga Prefecture's green environment conservation zones (area 1.78 ha; designated on August 30, 1989){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/21417.pdf |title=Shiga Environment 2009 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture |accessdate=2025-04-11}}. === Nyuu Shrine [Shimo-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Shimonyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Nagahama, Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu 1087 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 8 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509}} |神事 = Head Rite (January 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu. Like Kami-Nyuu, it is a disputed identification of an Engishiki shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 8). At the time of enshrinement it stood at the foot of Mt. Nyuho-Takayama, but it was moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 12). The former site still has a small shrine known as Kagurano Myojin. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 7). It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にゆうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にゆうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}. Since there are two shrines of the same name in the city, they are treated here as upper and lower sections. === Nyuu Shrine [Kami-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Kaminyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130}}Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu 378 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=two-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509}} |神事 = Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival (April 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto}} === Shrine crest === Circular mountain and water motif. === History === According to shrine tradition, around the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}, Nyuu no Mahito enshrined the Nyuu deity on Mt. Tanbono. It is said to have been moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 8). The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 3). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals === * Okonai - January 7 * Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival - April 2 * Annual festival - April 3 * Nogami Festival - August 23 === Shrine outside precincts === * Hachiman Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Sacred grove === The surrounding area is designated as the "Nyuu Shrine Sacred Grove" under Shiga Prefecture's green environment conservation zones (area 1.78 ha; designated on August 30, 1989){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/21417.pdf |title=Shiga Environment 2009 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture |accessdate=2025-04-11}}. === Nyuu Shrine [Shimo-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Shimonyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Nagahama, Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu 1087 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 8 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509}} |神事 = Head Rite (January 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu. Like Kami-Nyuu, it is a disputed identification of an Engishiki shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307}} 8). At the time of enshrinement it stood at the foot of Mt. Nyuho-Takayama, but it was moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 12). The former site still has a small shrine known as Kagurano Myojin. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 7). It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にゆうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Tanbono. It is said to have been moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577|1=1663}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals === * Okonai - January 7 * Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival - April 2 * Annual festival - April 3 * Nogami Festival - August 23 === Shrine outside precincts === * Hachiman Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Sacred grove === The surrounding area is designated as the "Nyuu Shrine Sacred Grove" under Shiga Prefecture's green environment conservation zones (area 1.78 ha; designated on August 30, 1989){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/21417.pdf |title=Shiga Environment 2009 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture |accessdate=2025-04-11}}. === Nyuu Shrine [Shimo-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Shimonyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Nagahama, Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu 1087 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Head Rite (January 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu. Like Kami-Nyuu, it is a disputed identification of an Engishiki shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). At the time of enshrinement it stood at the foot of Mt. Nyuho-Takayama, but it was moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 12). The former site still has a small shrine known as Kagurano Myojin. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7). It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にゆうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{特殊文字}} '''Nyuu Shrine''' (にゅうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Since there are two shrines of the same name in the city, they are treated here as upper and lower sections. === Nyuu Shrine [Kami-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Kaminyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu 378 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=two-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival (April 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} === Shrine crest === Circular mountain and water motif. === History === According to shrine tradition, around the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, Nyuu no Mahito enshrined the Nyuu deity on Mt. Tanbono. It is said to have been moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577|1=1663}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals === * Okonai - January 7 * Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival - April 2 * Annual festival - April 3 * Nogami Festival - August 23 === Shrine outside precincts === * Hachiman Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Sacred grove === The surrounding area is designated as the "Nyuu Shrine Sacred Grove" under Shiga Prefecture's green environment conservation zones (area 1.78 ha; designated on August 30, 1989){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/21417.pdf |title=Shiga Environment 2009 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture |accessdate=2025-04-11}}. === Nyuu Shrine [Shimo-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Shimonyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Nagahama, Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu 1087 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Head Rite (January 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu. Like Kami-Nyuu, it is a disputed identification of an Engishiki shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). At the time of enshrinement it stood at the foot of Mt. Nyuho-Takayama, but it was moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 12). The former site still has a small shrine known as Kagurano Myojin. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7). It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:にゆうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

Like Kami-Nyuu, it is a disputed identification of an Engishiki shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). At the time of enshrinement it stood at the foot of Mt. Nyuho-Takayama, but it was moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 12). The former site still has a small shrine known as Kagurano Myojin. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7). It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

{{特殊文字}} '''Nyuu Shrine''' (にゅうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Since there are two shrines of the same name in the city, they are treated here as upper and lower sections. === Nyuu Shrine [Kami-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Kaminyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu 378 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=two-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival (April 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} === Shrine crest === Circular mountain and water motif. === History === According to shrine tradition, around the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, Nyuu no Mahito enshrined the Nyuu deity on Mt. Tanbono. It is said to have been moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577|1=1663}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals === * Okonai - January 7 * Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival - April 2 * Annual festival - April 3 * Nogami Festival - August 23 === Shrine outside precincts === * Hachiman Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Sacred grove === The surrounding area is designated as the "Nyuu Shrine Sacred Grove" under Shiga Prefecture's green environment conservation zones (area 1.78 ha; designated on August 30, 1989){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/21417.pdf |title=Shiga Environment 2009 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture |accessdate=2025-04-11}}. === Nyuu Shrine [Shimo-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Shimonyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Nagahama, Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu 1087 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Head Rite (January 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu. Like Kami-Nyuu, it is a disputed identification of an Engishiki shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). At the time of enshrinement it stood at the foot of Mt. Nyuho-Takayama, but it was moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 12). The former site still has a small shrine known as Kagurano Myojin. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7). It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

{{特殊文字}} '''Nyuu Shrine''' (にゅうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Since there are two shrines of the same name in the city, they are treated here as upper and lower sections. === Nyuu Shrine [Kami-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Kaminyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu 378 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=two-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival (April 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} === Shrine crest === Circular mountain and water motif. === History === According to shrine tradition, around the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, Nyuu no Mahito enshrined the Nyuu deity on Mt. Tanbono. It is said to have been moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577|1=1663}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals === * Okonai - January 7 * Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival - April 2 * Annual festival - April 3 * Nogami Festival - August 23 === Shrine outside precincts === * Hachiman Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Sacred grove === The surrounding area is designated as the "Nyuu Shrine Sacred Grove" under Shiga Prefecture's green environment conservation zones (area 1.78 ha; designated on August 30, 1989){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/21417.pdf |title=Shiga Environment 2009 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture |accessdate=2025-04-11}}. === Nyuu Shrine [Shimo-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Shimonyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Nagahama, Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu 1087 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Head Rite (January 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu. Like Kami-Nyuu, it is a disputed identification of an Engishiki shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). At the time of enshrinement it stood at the foot of Mt. Nyuho-Takayama, but it was moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 12). The former site still has a small shrine known as Kagurano Myojin. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7). It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]][[Category:qq]]

{'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県長浜市余呉町上丹生378', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:丹生神社 (長浜市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11368606}} == Japanese content == {{特殊文字}} '''Nyuu Shrine''' (にゅうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Since there are two shrines of the same name in the city, they are treated here as upper and lower sections. === Nyuu Shrine [Kami-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Kaminyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu 378 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto
{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=two-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival (April 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|53.0|N|136|14|02.4|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Kami-Nyuu. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{ill|lt=disputed identification shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mitsuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} === Shrine crest === Circular mountain and water motif. === History === According to shrine tradition, around the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, Nyuu no Mahito enshrined the Nyuu deity on Mt. Tanbono. It is said to have been moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577|1=1663}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals === * Okonai - January 7 * Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival - April 2 * Annual festival - April 3 * Nogami Festival - August 23 === Shrine outside precincts === * Hachiman Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Sacred grove === The surrounding area is designated as the "Nyuu Shrine Sacred Grove" under Shiga Prefecture's green environment conservation zones (area 1.78 ha; designated on August 30, 1989){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/21417.pdf |title=Shiga Environment 2009 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture |accessdate=2025-04-11}}. === Nyuu Shrine [Shimo-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Shimonyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Nagahama, Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu 1087 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Head Rite (January 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu. Like Kami-Nyuu, it is a disputed identification of an Engishiki shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). At the time of enshrinement it stood at the foot of Mt. Nyuho-Takayama, but it was moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 12). The former site still has a small shrine known as Kagurano Myojin. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7). It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1663|Q7577|WD=Q7577|1=1663}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3). Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=village shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Festivals === * Okonai - January 7 * Hikiyama Tea Bowl Festival - April 2 * Annual festival - April 3 * Nogami Festival - August 23 === Shrine outside precincts === * Hachiman Shrine * Ebisu Shrine === Sacred grove === The surrounding area is designated as the "Nyuu Shrine Sacred Grove" under Shiga Prefecture's green environment conservation zones (area 1.78 ha; designated on August 30, 1989){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.shiga.lg.jp/file/attachment/21417.pdf |title=Shiga Environment 2009 |publisher=Shiga Prefecture |accessdate=2025-04-11}}. === Nyuu Shrine [Shimo-Nyuu] === {{神社 |名称 = Nyuu Shrine |画像 = [[File:Nyu-jinja (Shimonyu) 202308-1.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = Shiga Prefecture, Nagahama, Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu 1087 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}}
Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} / former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=one-bay nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}} |神事 = Head Rite (January 2) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Nyuu Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|33|24.8|N|136|13|40.8|E}}}} }} It is enshrined in Yogo-cho Shimo-Nyuu. Like Kami-Nyuu, it is a disputed identification of an Engishiki shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takaokami|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Niutsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=764|Q31163|WD=Q31163|1=764}} ({{ill|lt=Tenpyo-hoji|Q1346307|WD=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8). At the time of enshrinement it stood at the foot of Mt. Nyuho-Takayama, but it was moved to the present site in {{ill|lt=870|Q23629|WD=Q23629|1=870}} ({{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 12). The former site still has a small shrine known as Kagurano Myojin. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1667|Q7588|WD=Q7588|1=1667}} ({{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 7). It was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9). === Festivals === * Hana-no-kashira - January 2 * Hana-no-kashira - January 6 * O-gashira - January 14-15 * Annual festival - April 3 === Precinct shrines === * Ebisu Shrine * Inari Shrine === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Kaminyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Kami-Nyuu}} {{commonscat|Nyu-jinja (Nagahama, Shimonyu)|Nyuu Shrine in Shimo-Nyuu}} *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1273 Nyuu Shrine (Kami-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1272 Nyuu Shrine (Shimo-Nyuu)] (Shiga Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nyuu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]
